Skip to contents
intraitR 1.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

Package index

Landmark import and digitization

Read landmark data from tpsDig files, generic spreadsheets, or an interactive digitizer; visualise a single configuration.

read_tps()
Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file
read_landmarks_csv()
Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file
read_landmarks_xlsx()
Import landmark coordinates from a generic "wide"-format Excel sheet
digitize_landmarks()
Interactively digitize landmarks from specimen photographs
plot_landmarks()
Plot a single landmark configuration
print(<intrait_landmarks>)
Print an "intrait_landmarks" object

Superimposition and shape

Landmark quality control (missing-landmark imputation, orientation and geometry correction), Generalised Procrustes Analysis, allometric correction, and digitization/outlier quality control.

exclude_specimens()
Exclude known-bad specimens (e.g. mismeasured fish) from a landmark data set
impute_landmarks()
Impute missing (NA) landmark coordinates
standardize_orientation()
Standardize every specimen to the same head-left, belly-down orientation
correct_landmarks() print(<intrait_geometry_check>)
Manually correct a misplaced landmark using an alignment rule
correct_geometry()
Standardize landmark configurations to a common scale, orientation, and geometric convention
standardize_geometry()
Standardize landmark scale, scale-bar position, and rotation, without changing any measurement value
correct_geometry_conventions()
Actively correct landmarks that violate the FISHMORPH geometric conventions, once the axis is horizontal
fishmorph_shape_landmarks()
Extract complete-case body-shape landmarks from a FISHMORPH configuration
gpa_fish() print(<intrait_gpa>) summary(<intrait_gpa>) print(<summary.intrait_gpa>)
Generalised Procrustes Analysis for fish landmark configurations
correct_allometry()
Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry
detect_outliers() print(<intrait_outliers>)
Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample

Morphometric traits and ratios

Classical linear distances and ratios, including the FISHMORPH (Brosse et al., 2021) 11-measurement/9-ratio protocol.

linear_distances()
Compute inter-landmark linear distances
morpho_ratios()
Compute classical fish morphometric ratios
fishmorph_segments()
Compute linear morphological measurements following the FISHMORPH protocol
fishmorph_ratios()
Compute the nine FISHMORPH unitless ecomorphological ratios
plot_fishmorph_points()
Plot a specimen following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme
plot_fishmorph_shapes()
Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals

Functional and morphological space

Build, visualise, and test ordinations of shape or trait data, and quantify how intraspecific variability (ITV) affects estimated functional richness.

trait_space() print(<intrait_traitspace>)
Build a functional trait space from a numeric trait table
plot(<intrait_traitspace>)
Plot a functional trait space
plot_correlation_circle()
Plot the correlation circle of a functional trait space
morpho_space() print(<intrait_morphospace>)
Build a morphological space from Procrustes shape coordinates
plot(<intrait_morphospace>)
Plot a morphological space
trait_disparity()
Test differences in functional trait dispersion between groups
print(<intrait_disparity>)
Print an "intrait_disparity" object
bootstrap_functional_space()
Bootstrap-based estimate of functional space volume from individual data
print(<intrait_bootstrap_fspace>) plot(<intrait_bootstrap_fspace>)
Print and plot an "intrait_bootstrap_fspace" object
species_sensitivity()
Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates
print(<intrait_species_sensitivity>) plot(<intrait_species_sensitivity>)
Print and plot an "intrait_species_sensitivity" object
compare_functional_richness()
Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods
print(<intrait_richness_comparison>) plot(<intrait_richness_comparison>)
Print and plot an "intrait_richness_comparison" object
group_colors()
Look up the group/species colours used by the ordination plot methods
reset_group_colors()
Reset the session-level group/species colour cache

Phylogenetic data and ordination

A bundled reference phylogeny and a phylogenetic PCoA ordination producing axes directly usable as traits in trait_space(), for comparing functional and phylogenetic diversity loss.

load_fishmorph_phylogeny()
Bundled global fish phylogeny, ready to use with phylo_pcoa()
phylo_pcoa() print(<intrait_phylopcoa>)
Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis

Intraspecific variability and measurement error

Decompose trait variance into interspecific/intraspecific components, and quantify measurement and digitization error from replicated data.

itv_index() print(<intrait_itv>)
Partition trait variance into interspecific and intraspecific (ITV) components
plot(<intrait_itv>)
Plot the interspecific/intraspecific variance breakdown
intraspecific_variability() print(<intrait_variability>)
Quantify intraspecific morphological variability
measurement_error()
Estimate measurement error from replicated digitization
print(<intrait_measurement_error>)
Print an "intrait_measurement_error" object
digitization_error() print(<intrait_digitization_error>) plot(<intrait_digitization_error>)
Quantify hierarchical digitization (operator) error from repeated landmark placement, linear measurements, or ratios
summary_traits()
Summarise morphological traits by group

Simulated example data

simulate_fish_landmarks()
Simulate a fish landmark data set
simulate_fishmorph_points()
Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme

Real data: T-26 La Saudrune

A real electrofishing landmark data set (Adour-Garonne basin, France), used throughout the package’s examples and demo(pipeline_T26_saudrune).

load_t26_saudrune()
Real freshwater fish landmark data set from an electrofishing campaign (T-26, La Saudrune)
load_t26_saudrune_landmarks()
Real T-26 Saudrune landmark data, ready to use as an "intrait_landmarks" object

On this page
